task_completion.description: Blender is open and a 3D scene is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application window is visible, displaying the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: A UV sphere is added to the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spherical mesh object is visible in the 3D viewport, and/or 'Sphere' is listed in the Outliner panel (top right).

task_completion.description: The UV sphere has a radius of 1 unit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add UV Sphere' operator panel (bottom left) shows Radius: 1.0m, or the Item transform panel (sidebar) shows Dimensions of X: 2m, Y: 2m, Z: 2m (since radius 1 = diameter 2).

task_completion.description: The UV sphere is positioned at coordinates (0, -3, 2).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties tab or Item transform panel (sidebar) shows Location X: 0, Y: -3, Z: 2 for the selected sphere.

task_completion.description: The Blender file is saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/scene_with_sphere.blend.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar displays 'scene_with_sphere.blend', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/scene_with_sphere.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Blender GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender interface being used (menus, 3D viewport, properties panels) rather than a Python script being run in a terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The file was saved using Blender's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender 'Save As' dialog or the top menu 'File -> Save As' is seen being interacted with, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv'.

integrity.description: A real 3D mesh object was created in the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Outliner shows the 'Sphere' object with a mesh data icon (a triangle with vertices), proving it's not just a renamed empty or text object.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/scene_with_sphere.blend. Expected sphere name: 'Sphere' (Blender's default). Expected location: [0, -3, 2] with a tolerance of 1.0 units. Note: The default UV sphere radius in Blender is 1.0m, which corresponds to dimensions of 2m x 2m x 2m.
